jokes.text: Wait in line for a book?! You tell 'em Bart says hey.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to convince Bart to join book release line
jokes.punchline: Bart's dismissive attitude toward reading culture
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart dismissal of intellectual pursuits, good character consistency

jokes.text: I'm not a nerd. I'm a jock who's too cool for sports.
jokes.setup: Bart defending himself against nerd accusation
jokes.punchline: Paradoxical self-definition that makes no logical sense
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic delivered with complete confidence - peak Bart character comedy

jokes.text: I read a rumor Spider-man's in it.
jokes.setup: Nerds speculating about fantasy book content
jokes.punchline: Marvel character randomly inserted into Harry Potter-style series
jokes.explanation: Mild crossover absurdity, gets a chuckle but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I based my costume on the books, not the movies.
jokes.setup: Nerdy correction about Lisa's costume accuracy
jokes.punchline: Lisa's intellectual superiority and book purism
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Lisa's intellectual pretensions

jokes.text: Move or die!
jokes.setup: Organized book release with colored bracelets
jokes.punchline: Moe's violent interruption of civilized nerd gathering
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe moment - brief, violent, cuts through pretension

jokes.text: It's for my kid! Eureka!
jokes.setup: Moe seemingly being parental and responsible
jokes.punchline: Immediate selfish pivot to reading it himself
jokes.explanation: Classic Moe selfishness reveal

jokes.text: Do the character voices!
jokes.setup: Moe demanding Homer read aloud
jokes.punchline: Adult man demanding performance like a child
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing childishness from Moe

jokes.text: Maluicious Krubb is actually Kraluicious Mubb.
jokes.setup: Homer speed-reading through plot points
jokes.punchline: Nonsense character name reversal
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay, feels lazy

jokes.text: Magic, magic, magic, all is won.
jokes.setup: Homer rushing through fantasy novel climax
jokes.punchline: Reducing elaborate magical resolution to dismissive repetition
jokes.explanation: Good satire of fantasy genre conventions

jokes.text: But kids, I want you in bed by 3:00 a.m. Hey, somebody's got to be the bad guy.
jokes.setup: Homer setting a bedtime after midnight book release
jokes.punchline: 3 AM presented as strict parenting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parenting logic - absurdly permissive while claiming authority

jokes.text: Well, maybe he reached out done and killed someone.
jokes.setup: Mystery show about alibis and long-distance murder
jokes.punchline: Homer's garbled 'reached out and done' instead of standard phrase
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer malapropism, serviceable but not exceptional

jokes.text: Stop saying things, Bart. That's the TV's job.
jokes.setup: Bart making predictions about TV show
jokes.punchline: Homer's reverence for TV's authority over human commentary
jokes.explanation: Brilliant commentary on passive media consumption, very quotable

jokes.text: 'Cause only I can teach it and I ain't taught you, so you don't
jokes.setup: Dance instructor claiming exclusive teaching ability
jokes.punchline: Circular logic delivered with absolute confidence
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for pretentious dance teacher

jokes.text: Move over, Europe!
jokes.setup: Ballet academy coming to Springfield
jokes.punchline: Marge's ridiculous territorial challenge to an entire continent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge enthusiasm creating absurd global rivalry. Extremely economical and quotable.

jokes.text: Wednesday, you see I'm a heartless bastard. Thursday you realize you love me, damn it. Friday, we're closed.
jokes.setup: Dance academy schedule announcement
jokes.punchline: Escalating from business to emotional manipulation to mundane closure
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating structure with perfect anticlimatic ending

jokes.text: It's upstairs in my Disappointment Closet.
jokes.setup: Marge mentioning her Shattered Dreams Box
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of organized despair storage
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny concept delivered matter-of-factly, very memorable

jokes.text: doctor, safecracker, stethoscope sales lady
jokes.setup: Marge listing unfulfilled career dreams
jokes.punchline: Progression from noble to criminal to oddly specific mundane
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdity, stethoscope sales lady is delightfully random

jokes.text: My bosoms came in and ruined my balance.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking what ended Marge's ballet dreams
jokes.punchline: Blunt biological explanation for career failure
jokes.explanation: Marge's characteristic directness about awkward topics

jokes.text: They came in one at a time.
jokes.setup: Already established breast-related balance issues
jokes.punchline: Additional embarrassing detail making it worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes the original joke worse/funnier

jokes.text: I'm pretty sure you'll have your dad's boobs.
jokes.setup: Lisa worried about inheriting Marge's problem
jokes.punchline: Marge referencing Homer's physique as reassurance
jokes.explanation: Clever callback to Homer's appearance, good family genetics joke

jokes.text: You mean she danced well, into her 70s? Or she danced, well into her 70s?
jokes.setup: Marge citing Martha Graham as inspiration
jokes.punchline: Homer's unexpected grammatical precision about ambiguous phrasing
jokes.explanation: Surprising linguistic awareness from Homer, subverts expectations

jokes.text: Go-go or boring?
jokes.setup: Marge announcing she'll be a dancer
jokes.punchline: Homer's binary classification of dance types
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer reductionism, ballet as 'boring' dancing

jokes.text: Closed in a week. Too smart for the corndog crowd, too dumb for the bagel bunch.
jokes.setup: Chazz referencing his failed Broadway show
jokes.punchline: Class-based audience categorization with food stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant social commentary disguised as throwaway line, very quotable

jokes.text: You have a drinking problem? I said secret.
jokes.setup: Homer promising to reveal a family secret
jokes.punchline: Drinking problem is too obvious to be secret
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup-punchline structure, reveals character through humor

jokes.text: Gay out?
jokes.setup: Homer asking if Bart wonders what he does in locked room
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact assumption about Homer's secret
jokes.explanation: Mild shock humor, Bart's casual delivery sells it

jokes.text: The queen of all the jerkys!
jokes.setup: Homer revealing his secret jerky-making operation
jokes.punchline: Grandiose title for mundane snack food
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical food obsession with royal language

jokes.text: Some of that is cow blood.
jokes.setup: Father-son bonding moment over jerky making
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of disturbing ingredient
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly, typical Homer food grossness

jokes.text: It won't go down.
jokes.setup: Marge demonstrating her flexibility
jokes.punchline: Leg stuck in raised position, physical comedy
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy, visual gag inferred from dialogue

jokes.text: How can they teach if they can't do? Get out!
jokes.setup: Discussing the saying 'those who cannot do, teach'
jokes.punchline: Chazz's literal interpretation and immediate ejection
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chazz's ego and illogic

jokes.text: This is how I always stand when I tell adults what they should be doing.
jokes.setup: Chazz commenting on Lisa's posture
jokes.punchline: Lisa's self-aware acknowledgment of her bossy nature
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-aware character moment, Lisa acknowledging her trademark behavior

jokes.text: married your plastic surgeon, retired, and then un-retired in a special little show called Woman of the Year
jokes.setup: Chazz listing Broadway diva lifecycle
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific show business clichés ending in real show reference
jokes.explanation: Good escalating satire of Broadway career paths

jokes.text: We balerinas are under constant pressure to stay focused, skinny, graceful and skinny.
jokes.setup: Explaining pressures of ballet
jokes.punchline: Repeating 'skinny' emphasizes obsession with weight
jokes.explanation: Good repetition for emphasis, highlights ballet's unhealthy aspects

jokes.text: But for every ballerina in the world, it's cigarettes.
jokes.setup: Different coping mechanisms like yoga and meditation
jokes.punchline: Universal smoking addiction among ballerinas
jokes.explanation: Dark humor subverting expectations, good setup-punchline structure

jokes.text: Zack's life wasn't so sweet then.
jokes.setup: Lisa referencing Disney Channel show about smoking
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'Suite Life' title becoming 'sweet life'
jokes.explanation: Mild pun, shows Lisa's earnest reference to children's TV

jokes.text: If God didn't want ballerinas to smoke, how come I can do this?
jokes.setup: Religious justification for smoking
jokes.punchline: Visual demonstration of smoking while dancing
jokes.explanation: Absurd logic typical of addiction rationalization

jokes.text: Maybe it's all the secondhand 'focus' and 'pep' you are inhaling.
jokes.setup: Lisa's sudden improvement in dancing
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic description of secondhand smoke effects
jokes.explanation: Clever euphemism for harmful substances as beneficial

jokes.text: I thought they were cancer sticks. I dan't dear dou!
jokes.setup: Lisa calling cigarettes 'cancer sticks'
jokes.punchline: Ballerina's speech impediment from cigarette in mouth
jokes.explanation: Simple speech impediment gag, fairly predictable

jokes.text: Step into our Jerkatorium.
jokes.setup: Homer showing off jerky operation to Apu
jokes.punchline: Pretentious Latin-style name for jerky-making room
jokes.explanation: Good portmanteau showing Homer's grandiose food pretensions

jokes.text: You have made a very powerless enemy.
jokes.setup: Apu threatening Homer after jerky theft
jokes.punchline: Admission of impotence while making threat
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of threat cliché, perfect Apu character moment

jokes.text: You can skip the 'K' and the 'Y.'
jokes.setup: Bart doing JERKY acronym presentation
jokes.punchline: Apu cutting off pointless business pitch
jokes.explanation: Good interruption of tedious presentation, Apu's practical dismissal

jokes.text: As the oak said to the beagle, you're barkin' up the wrong tree.
jokes.setup: Ned defending himself from Homer's jerky accusation
jokes.punchline: Elaborate setup to deliver common idiom pun
jokes.explanation: Typical Flanders wordplay, character-appropriate but not exceptional

jokes.text: I spent the whole morning blacking out the 'goshes' and 'darns' in these Hardy Boys books.
jokes.setup: Ned establishing his alibi
jokes.punchline: Censoring extremely mild language in children's books
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flanders extreme prudishness, absurd level of censorship

jokes.text: Ah! 'heck, darn.' I don't think so.
jokes.setup: Flanders censoring books
jokes.punchline: His shocked reaction to mild language
jokes.explanation: Classic Flanders prudishness. Character-perfect but predictable for his established personality.

jokes.text: I don't think so.
jokes.setup: Reading censored 'heck, darn' in book
jokes.punchline: Ned's disapproval of his own inadequate censorship
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Ned's impossible standards even for himself

jokes.text: You mean losers like... Simone de Beauvoir, Margaret Mead, Queen Elizabeth I, Lauren Bacall...
jokes.setup: Lisa calling smokers losers
jokes.punchline: List of accomplished historical women who smoked
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection using historical figures, subverts anti-smoking message

jokes.text: Bring out the Hellman's, and bring out the best.
jokes.setup: Lillian Hellman encouraging Lisa to smoke
jokes.punchline: Pun on mayonnaise slogan using playwright's name
jokes.explanation: Clever pun but somewhat forced, relies on name similarity

jokes.text: I brought the best weapon to operate while drugged: a crossbow.
jokes.setup: Homer preparing to fight raccoons while on sleeping pills
jokes.punchline: Crossbow as optimal impaired weapon choice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic, Homer's twisted practical thinking

jokes.text: Lisa the dancing Marge girl.
jokes.setup: Homer confusing Lisa with Marge about dancing
jokes.punchline: Garbled correction that's still wrong
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer confusion, shows his scattered thinking

jokes.text: That wasn't a defeat, it was a phased withdrawal.
jokes.setup: Bart mentioning Homer lost war with worms
jokes.punchline: Military euphemism for obvious defeat
jokes.explanation: Good euphemism joke, Homer's inability to admit failure

jokes.text: When you cut 'em, they multiply. I can't fight that!
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his defeat to worms
jokes.punchline: Logical reason for surrender against regenerating enemies
jokes.explanation: Good callback explaining the previous joke, Homer's practical cowardice

jokes.text: Look, there's a Homer and a Bart and a Marge and a Lisa.
jokes.setup: Raccoon family eating jerky
jokes.punchline: Homer anthropomorphizing raccoons as family mirror
jokes.explanation: Sweet visual gag showing Homer's sentimental nature

jokes.text: and, one day, you'll grow up to be people.
jokes.setup: Homer addressing raccoon family lovingly
jokes.punchline: Belief that raccoons will evolve into humans
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic failure, sweet but completely wrong

jokes.text: When the big cigarette hits 9.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking when their next break is
jokes.punchline: Clock made of cigarette instead of normal timepiece
jokes.explanation: Creative visual gag showing smoking culture dominance

jokes.text: Hit the river, ladies. Go ahead, have a wallow.
jokes.setup: Chazz dismissing dancers for break
jokes.punchline: Comparing ballerinas to hippos in water
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier 'hippopotamus' insult, Chazz's consistent cruelty

jokes.text: A 'sturgeon' is a fish. And a very wise fish he is!
jokes.setup: Homer misunderstanding 'Surgeon General' as 'sturgeon'
jokes.punchline: Doubling down on fish authority figure
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer malapropism with confident follow-through

jokes.text: Oh, fatherhood hurts my brain.
jokes.setup: Homer torn between protecting Lisa and keeping Marge happy
jokes.punchline: Homer's mental exhaustion from parental responsibility
jokes.explanation: Relatable parental frustration delivered in Homer's voice

jokes.text: I'm gonna stick to you like waffle syrup on a shag rug.
jokes.setup: Bart assigned to watch Lisa
jokes.punchline: Overly specific and disgusting simile for surveillance
jokes.explanation: Creative gross-out simile, shows Bart's colorful language

jokes.text: I'm gonna be on you like fish stink on a Gloucester dog.
jokes.setup: Bart continuing his surveillance threats
jokes.punchline: Even more specific regional reference
jokes.explanation: Escalation that's less effective than the first simile

jokes.text: Hey, I'm still paying for that nose!
jokes.setup: Dancer hitting Chazz in the face during rehearsal
jokes.punchline: Chazz's vanity about expensive plastic surgery
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Chazz's superficiality

jokes.text: If you don't breathe it, that smoke might go into a baby.
jokes.setup: Lisa resisting peer pressure to smoke
jokes.punchline: Twisted logic about preventing harm to infants
jokes.explanation: Brilliant twisted peer pressure logic, subverts anti-smoking message

jokes.text: Wrong number.
jokes.setup: Homer having dramatic phone conversation about catching Lisa
jokes.punchline: Dismissing obvious setup as wrong number
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of dramatic moment, typical Homer deflection

jokes.text: Not keys, cigarettes!
jokes.setup: Raccoon supposed to steal cigarettes
jokes.punchline: Lisa's frustrated correction about the wrong theft
jokes.explanation: Functional escalation of the plan going wrong. Basic mistaken object humor.

jokes.text: Yeah, that's what I mean.
jokes.setup: Raccoon bringing keys instead of cigarettes
jokes.punchline: Bart pretending the wrong item was intentional
jokes.explanation: Weak cover-up joke, Bart trying to save face

jokes.text: Can't. I'm pregnant.
jokes.setup: Woman asking for cigarette during smoking withdrawal
jokes.punchline: Medical reason for not smoking during crisis
jokes.explanation: Solid setup-punchline about smoking addiction priorities

jokes.text: That's your finger!
jokes.setup: Desperate ballerina finding 'cigarette'
jokes.punchline: Attempting to smoke own finger in withdrawal desperation
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy showing extreme withdrawal desperation

jokes.text: I'll spit on your grave, grandma!
jokes.setup: Ballerinas fighting over cigarettes
jokes.punchline: Extreme threat against elderly person
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation showing complete breakdown of civility

jokes.text: Crap Like This. Ran for five years.
jokes.setup: Chazz calling recital 'crap like this'
jokes.punchline: His show with same name was actually successful
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to earlier Broadway references, ironic success

jokes.text: It stunts your growth. And it's marketed to children. I'm talking about ballet.
jokes.setup: Marge describing harmful practice that sounds like smoking
jokes.punchline: Reveal that she's attacking ballet instead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection subverting audience expectations, excellent structure

jokes.text: You'll never take down big ballet! Never!
jokes.setup: Marge's anti-ballet speech
jokes.punchline: Chazz defending 'big ballet' as corporate conspiracy
jokes.explanation: Good parody of 'big tobacco' rhetoric applied to ballet

jokes.text: Does that mean I can stop training to be a Mexican wrestler?
jokes.setup: Marge realizes living through children is wrong
jokes.punchline: Reveals Bart has been forced into equally ridiculous pursuit
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing the scope of Marge's vicarious living. Mexican wrestling is perfectly absurd for Bart.

jokes.text: No! Now practice preening to the crowd, El Guapo.
jokes.setup: Bart asking if he can stop Mexican wrestling training
jokes.punchline: Homer's continued projection of his dreams onto children
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Homer hasn't learned lesson

jokes.text: You feed on their hatred and you are so hungry!
jokes.setup: Homer coaching Bart in heel wrestling persona
jokes.punchline: Dramatic metaphor for villain performance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's wrestling coaching obsession
